prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `66f2d22f`.
codex_response:
The rule is: split the 4x14 input into two 4x7 halves and output a 4x7 grid with `5` exactly where both halves have `0`; all other output cells stay `0`. The Opus summary matched the official examples, so I used it; the Sonnet file had no entry for this task. No new shared primitive was needed.

I added the task package at [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66f2d22f/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66f2d22f/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66f2d22f/generator.py#L26). The generator explicitly builds two monochrome 4x7 panels, gives them a shared zero-mask plus panel-specific extra holes, and constrains row hole counts so the generated inputs stay close to the official distribution.

Validation passed end to end:
- Official examples: `5/5` passed via `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 66f2d22f`
- Generated examples: `1000/1000` passed via `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 66f2d22f`
- I also sanity-checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/previews/preview_01.png) visually and it looks on-family

Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/66f2d22f/previews/preview_03.png).
